diagrams.title: IPv4 vs IPv6 Header Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing fields in both protocol headers
elements: IPv4: Version(4), IHL(4), TOS(8), Total Length(16), Identification(16), Flags(3), Fragment Offset(13), TTL(8), Protocol(8), Checksum(16), Source IP(32), Dest IP(32), Options
IPv6: Version(4), Traffic Class(8), Flow Label(20), Payload Length(16), Next Header(8), Hop Limit(8), Source IP(128), Dest IP(128), Extension Headers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding structural differences and why IPv6 processes faster

diagrams.title: IPv4 Address Classes
diagrams.description: Visual representation of Class A, B, C, D, E address ranges
diagrams.purpose: Memorizing classful addressing boundaries and special ranges

diagrams.title: IPv6 Address Types Flowchart
diagrams.description: Decision diagram for identifying IPv6 address types from prefix
elements: Start -> Check first hex digit(s) -> 0 (::1 loopback), 1-9 (global unicast 2000::/3), f (fe80-feb link-local, ff (multicast), fd/fc (unique local)
diagrams.purpose: Quick identification of IPv6 address categories based on prefix

diagrams.title: NAT Operation Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows how Network Address Translation maps private to public addresses
elements: Private Network (192.168.1.x) -> NAT Router -> Public IP (203.0.113.5) -> Internet
NAT Table: Internal IP:Port <-> External IP:Port mapping
PAT (Port Address Translation) uses unique port numbers for each connection
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how NAT enables internet access for private network hosts
